task_objective: Determine OOM drivers for large datasets and implement performance/memory heuristics
design_decisions: Separated data generation from profiling in benchmarks to isolate memory overhead.
Implemented a new `MemoryManager` class to handle dynamic batch sizing and DuckDB memory limits.
Batched the 'Advanced Pass' (Skewness/MAD) in the Profiler, which was previously a major OOM driver.
Reduced the default `global_batch_size` from 500 to a dynamic heuristic (typically 20-100 for large datasets).
Set DuckDB `memory_limit` to 70% of available system RAM automatically.
Added an `analysis` property to the compatibility wrapper to support performance tracking.
results: Successfully profiled 20M x 20cols in ~77 seconds with peak RAM stabilized at ~2.6GB (compared to previous OOMs). Benchmarks confirmed that smaller batches have minimal performance impact (~5%) but provide significant memory safety on large datasets.
